headline: Public University Intake: 89% Bumiputera Despite 68% Population Share
context: Education Ministry data shows public university undergraduate intake in 2024 was 89% Bumiputera, against a 68% Bumiputera population share, with Chinese and Indian representation at historic lows.

cards.big: 89% of public university seats go to Bumiputera students.
cards.sub: Bumiputera are 68% of the population. The 21-point gap has widened from 14 points in 2010.

cards.big: STPM holders require higher grades than Matriculation holders for the same course.
cards.sub: STPM is predominantly taken by non-Bumiputera. Matriculation is 90% reserved for Bumiputera.

cards.big: Chinese student representation in public universities fell from 26% in 2000 to 8% in 2024.
cards.sub: The decline has pushed 72% of Chinese students into private institutions at family expense.

cards.big: India's affirmative action reserves 50% of seats — not 89%.
cards.sub: India's Supreme Court ruled in 1992 that reservations exceeding 50% are unconstitutional.

cards.big: When representation exceeds population share by 21 points, the system is not correcting imbalance. It is creating a new one.

Affirmative action in education should lift the disadvantaged without excluding the qualified. A system where STPM straight-A students are rejected while lower-scoring matriculation students enter is not meritocracy or equity.
